The '''San-Ni staff''' was a [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapon]] favored by [[Martial artist/Legends|martial artists]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including [[Jedi Weapon Master]]s who used the weapon as an alternative to the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. It consisted of three metal rods held together by [[Power coupling/Legends|power couplings]].

==Description==
A difficult weapon to use effectively, yet popular among its few users for its discreet nature. The staff, when deactivated resembled a baton 50 centimeters in length. It could be activated only by first grabbing each half of the baton, twisting, then pulling them apart. A 10 centimeter long stun prod then sprang from each end of the staff, and two high-voltage power couplings would crackle to life in between the three pieces. Activated, the San-Ni tripled in length to 150 centimeters. The electrical discharge was powerful enough to shear off limbs, cook flesh, and even a near miss would give severe burns to an enemy, the wielder however seemed to be shielded from this, through the use of miniature heat sinks.

The San-Ni staff was a versatile weapon. The wielder could protect himself from [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] blows by parrying them using the san-ni staff's power couplings, which were impervious to lightsaber damage. In addition, a wielder could choose to strike an opponent using the power couplings in order to deal non-lethal [[Stun/Legends|stunning]] damage.<ref name="JATM" />

==History==
The San-Ni staff was used by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Waldan Bridger]], a trait for which he was most known for in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. He employed its use in a duel with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] during the [[Battle of Togoria]] during the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. However, it did not stop the cyborg from cutting the warrior down within the first minute of the quarrel.
